title: Oscilllating jig saw blade
abstract: An accessory tool for an oscillating power tool includes a support arm portion having a first lateral edge and a second lateral edge and a blade portion extending from a forward end portion of the support arm portion generally in a lateral direction. The blade portion includes a cutting edge arranged facing in the forward direction and a trailing edge that faces toward the mounting portion. The mounting portion defines a central axis which intersects the oscillating axis when the mounting portion is attached to a tool holder of the oscillating power tool. The first lateral edge of the support arm portion and the trailing edge of the blade portion define an open region which is interposed between the blade portion and the mounting portion. The central axis intersects the open region and both the trailing edge and the cutting edge of the blade portion.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1 . An accessory tool for an oscillating power tool, the oscillating power tool defining an oscillating axis, the accessory tool comprising: a mounting portion configured to mate with an oscillating drive member of a power tool; a support arm portion extending from the mounting portion in a forward direction, the support arm portion including a first lateral edge and a second lateral edge; and a blade portion extending from a forward end portion of the support arm portion generally in a lateral direction, the blade portion including a cutting edge arranged facing in the forward direction and a trailing edge that faces toward the mounting portion, wherein the mounting portion defines a central axis which intersects the oscillating axis when the mounting portion is attached to a tool holder of the oscillating power tool, wherein the first lateral edge of the support arm portion and the trailing edge of the blade portion define an open region which is interposed between the blade portion and the mounting portion, and wherein the central axis intersects the open region and both the trailing edge and the cutting edge of the blade portion. 2. The accessory tool of claim 1 , wherein the cutting edge is arranged perpendicular to the central axis. 3. The accessory tool of claim 1 , wherein the blade portion includes an attached portion which is attached to the forward end portion of the support arm portion, and wherein the first lateral edge is angled toward the second lateral edge and toward the attached portion of the blade such that the support arm portion narrows as the support arm portion approaches the blade portion. 4. The accessory tool of claim 1 , wherein the mounting portion includes a transition region from which the support arm portion extends, the transition region offsetting the support arm portion from the mounting portion. 5. The accessory tool of claim 1 , wherein the trailing edge and the cutting edge meet to define a tip portion at an opposite end of the blade portion from the attached portion, and wherein the trailing edge and the cutting edge are tapered toward each other such that the blade portion narrows as it approaches the tip portion. 6. The accessory tool of claim 1 , wherein the first lateral edge includes a lip portion, the lip portion configured to serve as a guide surface for the accessory tool during cutting operations. 7. The accessory tool of claim 1 , wherein the mounting portion is formed of a first metal plate and the blade portion is formed of a second metal plate. 8. An oscillating power tool comprising: an oscillating drive member that defines an oscillating axis; a tool holder fixed to the oscillating drive member; an accessory tool releasably retained by the tool holder, the accessory tool including: a mounting portion configured to mate with an oscillating drive member of a power tool; a support arm portion extending from the mounting portion in a forward direction, the support arm portion including a first lateral edge and a second lateral edge; and a blade portion extending from a forward end portion of the support arm portion generally in a lateral direction, the blade portion including a cutting edge arranged facing in the forward direction and a trailing edge that faces toward the mounting portion, wherein the mounting portion defines a central axis which intersects the oscillating axis, wherein the first lateral edge of the support arm portion and the trailing edge of the blade portion define an open region which is interposed between the blade portion and the mounting portion, and wherein the central axis intersects the open region and both the trailing edge and the cutting edge of the blade portion. 9. The oscillating power tool of claim 8, wherein the cutting edge is arranged perpendicular to the central axis. 10. The oscillating power tool of claim 8, wherein the blade portion includes an attached portion which is attached to the forward end portion of the support arm portion, and wherein the first lateral edge is angled toward the second lateral edge and toward the attached portion of the blade such that the support arm portion narrows as the support arm portion approaches the blade portion. 1 1 . The oscillating power tool of claim 8, wherein the mounting portion includes a transition region from which the support arm portion extends, the transition region offsetting the support arm portion from the mounting portion. 12. The oscillating power tool of claim 8, wherein the trailing edge and the cutting edge meet to define a tip portion at an opposite end of the blade portion from the attached portion, and wherein the trailing edge and the cutting edge are tapered toward each other such that the blade portion narrows as it approaches the tip portion. 13. The oscillating power tool of claim 8, wherein the first lateral edge includes a lip portion, the lip portion configured to serve as a guide surface for the accessory tool during cutting operations. 14. The oscillating power tool of claim 8, wherein the mounting portion is formed of a first metal plate and the blade portion is formed of a second metal plate.
